title: Windmannia elliptica
taxonomy: Windmannia elliptica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Stemonuraceae. It belongs to the genus Windmannia, which is a small group of plants. The plant is native to tropical Africa. The classification is based on morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. intro: Windmannia elliptica, also known as the Windmannia vine, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Windmannia elliptica include sesquiterpenoids and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve symptoms of fever, arthritis, and digestive disorders.
